prompts.text: The old house at the end of the street had always been

prompts.label: Advice
prompts.text: The best way to learn something new is to

0.1: mysterious. Nobody in the neighbourhood ever saw the lights on.
mysterious. Nobody in the neighbourhood ever saw the lights on.
mysterious. Nobody in the neighbourhood had ever seen lights on inside.
mysterious. Not one person on the street had ever seen anyone go in.
mysterious. Nobody in the neighbourhood ever saw anyone come or go.
0.5: mysterious. Nobody in the neighbourhood had ever seen the lights on.
abandoned. The garden was overgrown and the windows were cracked.
mysterious. Children dared each other to knock, but none ever did.
abandoned. Its garden was wild and no one had lived there for years.
mysterious. Curtains twitched sometimes, but never when you were looking.
1.0: abandoned. The garden had long since returned to wilderness.
mysterious, even to those who had grown up in its shadow.
a source of fascination and quiet dread for the children on Maple Close.
empty — or so everyone assumed, until the morning the lights came on.
the subject of rumours. Some said a widow lived there. Others said worse.
1.5: a puzzle no one had ever quite solved, despite living next door for decades.
draped in fog, even on clear days — a local weather anomaly nobody questioned.
the place where lost things went: mittens, cats, the occasional bicycle.
avoided, intuitively, by every postman who'd ever worked the route.
where the neighbourhood children claimed you could hear a piano, faintly, at midnight.
2.0: quietly wrong — not haunted, exactly, but incorrectly angled with respect to the sun.
a mystery wrapped in peeling paint and profound municipal indifference.
the kind of place that made pigeons reroute instinctively.
where Theodora Vine-Markham had once conducted unlicensed experiments in apiculture.
spoken of in the conditional tense: 'that house has always been about to fall down.'

0.1: practise regularly and be patient with yourself.
0.5: start immediately and not overthink it.
break it into small steps and practise each one.
start with the basics and build up gradually.
1.0: dive in and make lots of mistakes early.
find someone who already knows it and ask them to teach you.
practise a little every day rather than cramming.
get your hands dirty — theory only takes you so far.
connect it to something you already know and love.
1.5: teach it to someone else before you've fully learned it yourself.
sleep on it — literally. Consolidation happens during deep sleep.
find the most boring part and master that first.
change your environment every time you study it.
give yourself a week, then deliberately forget it, then relearn.
2.0: pay attention to the moment just before you give up.
decide it doesn't matter — then notice you've learned it anyway.
stop trying to learn it and just use it badly.
find the person who hates it most and ask them why.

0.1: vast library where everything is available, but finding it is the challenge.
vast library where everything is available, but finding it takes skill.
vast library where all human knowledge is stored, but navigation is key.
vast library with everything ever written — but the filing system is chaos.
vast library where the information is free but the map is missing.
0.5: vast library where everything is available, but the filing system is chaotic.
city that never sleeps — full of wonders, dangers, and back alleys.
ocean: vast, interconnected, and easy to drown in if you're not careful.
massive library with no librarian and a thousand people shouting at once.
parallel city built entirely of words, images, and attention.
1.0: vast ocean — mostly unexplored, occasionally beautiful, occasionally treacherous.
mirror: it shows you what you already believe, reflected back more loudly.
library built by everyone, curated by no one, and on fire in several places.
city with a million neighbourhoods, most of which you'll never find.
megaphone given to every human simultaneously, for better and worse.
1.5: geological strata: layers of past conversations slowly compressing into something new.
collective dream we've agreed to take seriously.
very old pub that has been extended so many times no one knows the original walls.
second mind that runs in parallel and shares your anxieties.
long argument nobody started and nobody will ever finish.
2.0: vast filing cabinet held together with the ambient anxiety of strangers.
conversation between people who've never met and never will.
recursive question that generates more questions than it answers.
place where both cures and curses arrive in the same envelope.
planet-sized bulletin board pinned with notes from eight billion people, mostly unsigned.
